---
title: 'Figures and Tables: Assessing the Utility of the EAU Risk Stratification for Predicting
  Kidney Stone Recurrence'
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
    fig_caption: false
    keep_tex: false
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '2'
    df_print: paged
header-includes:
- \usepackage{graphicx}
- \usepackage{float}
- \usepackage{booktabs}
- \usepackage{multirow}
- \usepackage{array}
- \usepackage{longtable}
- \usepackage{makecell}
- \usepackage{pdflscape}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(meta)
library(metafor)
library(scales)
library(ggh4x)
```

\newpage
# Tables

## Table 1. High Risk Conditions as per EAU Urolithiasis Guidelines

List of conditions associated with 'high risk' disease as per EAU Urolithiasis guidelines i.e. if an individual has any one of the scenarios/conditions detailed in this table then they are labelled as high risk. Availability in each Biobank is detailed. UKB = UK Biobank, AoU = All of Us.

\begin{landscape}
\begin{longtable}{p{3.5cm}p{8cm}cc}
\toprule
\textbf{Category} & \textbf{Factor} & \textbf{Availability in UKB} & \textbf{Availability in AoU} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\toprule
\textbf{Category} & \textbf{Factor} & \textbf{Availability in UKB} & \textbf{Availability in AoU} \\
\midrule
\endhead
\multirow{8}{*}{\makecell[l]{General factors}} & Early onset of urolithiasis (especially children and teenagers) & Age at first stone <25 & Age at first stone <25 \\
& Familial stone formation & Data not available & Data not available \\
& Recurrent stone formers & Available & Available \\
& Short time since last stone episode & Available & Available \\
& Brushite-containing stones (CaHPO4.2H2O) & Stone analysis not available & Stone analysis not available \\
& Uric acid and urate-containing stones & Stone analysis not available & Stone analysis not available \\
& Infection stones & Stone analysis not available & Stone analysis not available \\
& Solitary kidney & Available & Available \\
\midrule
\multirow{10}{*}{\makecell[l]{Diseases associated\\with stone formation}} & Hyperparathyroidism & Available & Available \\
& Metabolic syndrome & Available & Available \\
& Nephrocalcinosis & Available & Available \\
& Polycystic kidney disease (PKD) & Available & Available \\
& GI diseases (jejuno-ileal bypass, intestinal resection, Crohn's disease, malabsorptive conditions, enteric hyperoxaluria after urinary diversion, exocrine pancreatic insufficiency) and bariatric surgery & Available & Available \\
& Increased levels of vitamin D & Available & Available \\
& Sarcoidosis & Available & Available \\
& Spinal cord injury, neurogenic bladder & Available & Available \\
& Ureteral stricture & Available & Available \\
\midrule
\multirow{7}{*}{\makecell[l]{Genetically determined\\stone formation}} & Cystinuria (type A, B and AB) & Available & Available \\
& Primary hyperoxaluria (PH) & Available & Available \\
& Renal tubular acidosis (RTA) type I & Available & Available \\
& 2,8-Dihydroxyadeninuria & Available & Available \\
& Xanthinuria & Available & Available \\
& Lesch-Nyhan syndrome & Available & Available \\
& Cystic fibrosis & Available & Available \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Drug-induced stone formation} & & \\
\midrule
\multirow{6}{*}{\makecell[l]{Anatomical abnormalities\\associated with stone\\formation}} & Medullary sponge kidney (tubular ectasia) & Available & Available \\
& Ureteropelvic junction (UPJ) obstruction & Available & Available \\
& Calyceal diverticulum, calyceal cyst & Available & Available \\
& Vesico-uretero-renal reflux & Available & Available \\
& Horseshoe kidney & Available & Available \\
& Ureterocele & Available & Available \\
\midrule
\multirow{2}{*}{\makecell[l]{Environmental and\\professional factors}} & High ambient temperatures & Not available & Not available \\
& Chronic lead and cadmium exposure & Not available & Not available \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}
\end{landscape}

\newpage

## Table 2. Demographics

Demographics table for those with clinically significant kidney stone disease within the two Biobanks utilised for the present study. \textsuperscript{\#} At time of first stone event. * Derived from Kaplan-Meier survival curves on the 'any event' "virtual stone clinic" dataset (every participant contributes one row per transition they reach: 1→2, 2→3, 3→4, 4→5).

```{r table2-vsc-rates, include=FALSE}
# Load the virtual-stone-clinic KM rates (one row per horizon × cohort)
# produced by the source scripts (UKB Rmd / AoU notebook).
ukb_vsc <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/virtual_stone_clinic_recurrence_rates.csv",
                    check.names = FALSE)
aou_vsc <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/virtual_stone_clinic_recurrence_rates_aou.csv",
                    check.names = FALSE)

fmt_vsc <- function(df, h) {
  row <- df[df$horizon_years == h, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(row) == 0 || !is.finite(row$rec_pct)) return("--")
  sprintf("%.0f\\%% (%.0f--%.0f\\%%)",
          row$rec_pct, row$rec_lo_pct, row$rec_hi_pct)
}

vsc_2_ukb  <- fmt_vsc(ukb_vsc, 2)
vsc_5_ukb  <- fmt_vsc(ukb_vsc, 5)
vsc_10_ukb <- fmt_vsc(ukb_vsc, 10)
vsc_2_aou  <- fmt_vsc(aou_vsc, 2)
vsc_5_aou  <- fmt_vsc(aou_vsc, 5)
vsc_10_aou <- fmt_vsc(aou_vsc, 10)
```

\begin{table}[H]
\centering
\small
\begin{tabular}{llcc}
\toprule
& & \textbf{UK Biobank} & \textbf{All of Us} \\
& & \textbf{(n=8,375)} & \textbf{(n=11,182)} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Age at First clinically significant stone event, mean $\pm$SD} & 52$\pm$11 & 52$\pm$15 \\
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Men, n (\%)} & 5,850 (70\%) & 5,038 (45\%) \\
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Follow-up, median years (IQR)} & 21.9 (15.0--40.4) & 7.3 (5.0--7.5) \\
\multicolumn{2}{l}{High Risk as per EAU\textsuperscript{\#}, n (\%)} & 3,103 (37\%) & 2,032 (18.2\%) \\
\midrule
\multirow{5}{*}{\makecell[l]{Initial Presentation/\\Intervention, n (\%)}} & PCNL & 471 (6\%) & 186 (2\%) \\
& ESWL & 1,287 (15\%) & 380 (3\%) \\
& URS & 207 (3\%) & 2,410 (21\%) \\
& Open surgery & 18 (0.2\%) & 38 (0.3\%) \\
& Ureteric Colic & 6,392 (76\%) & 7,342 (66\%) \\
\midrule
\multirow{3}{*}{\makecell[l]{Recurrence Rates,\\\% (95\% CI)*}} & 2-Year  & `r vsc_2_ukb`  & `r vsc_2_aou`  \\
& 5-Year  & `r vsc_5_ukb`  & `r vsc_5_aou`  \\
& 10-Year & `r vsc_10_ukb` & `r vsc_10_aou` \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\newpage

## Supplementary Table 1. Numbers of Patients with High-Risk Characteristics at First event

Numbers of patients with High-Risk characteristics at time of first stone event - for each analysis risk status is (re)ascertained at the point of presentation. NB -- patients can have more than one characteristic.

\begin{landscape}
\begin{longtable}{p{3.5cm}p{8cm}cc}
\toprule
\textbf{Category} & \textbf{Factor} & \textbf{UKB, n (\%)} & \textbf{AoU, n (\%)} \\
& & \textbf{Total n = 8,375} & \textbf{Total n = 11,182} \\
\midrule
\endfirsthead
\toprule
\textbf{Category} & \textbf{Factor} & \textbf{UKB, n (\%)} & \textbf{AoU, n (\%)} \\
\midrule
\endhead
\multirow{9}{*}{\makecell[l]{General factors}} & Early onset of urolithiasis & 83 (1\%) & 479 (4\%) \\
& Familial stone formation & Data not available & Data not available \\
& Recurrent stone formers & See data above & See data above \\
& Short time since last stone episode & See data above & See data above \\
& Brushite-containing stones & Stone analysis not available & Stone analysis not available \\
& Uric acid and urate-containing stones & Stone analysis not available & Stone analysis not available \\
& Infection stones & Stone analysis not available & Stone analysis not available \\
& Solitary kidney & 168 (0.9\%) & 4 (0.04\%) \\
& Immunosuppression & 38 (0.4\%) & 181 (2\%) \\
\midrule
\multirow{9}{*}{\makecell[l]{Diseases associated\\with stone formation}} & Hyperparathyroidism & 171 (2\%) & 374 (3\%) \\
& Metabolic syndrome & 2,317 (27\%) & 254 (2\%) \\
& Nephrocalcinosis & 12 (0.1\%) & 20 (0.2\%) \\
& Polycystic kidney disease (PKD) & 53 (0.6\%) & 58 (0.5\%) \\
& GI diseases and bariatric surgery & 527 (6\%) & 953 (9\%) \\
& Increased levels of vitamin D & 0 & 0 \\
& Sarcoidosis & 45 (0.5\%) & 84 (1\%) \\
& Spinal cord injury, neurogenic bladder & 111 (1.3\%) & 155 (1\%) \\
& Ureteral stricture & 325 (4\%) & 106 (1\%) \\
\midrule
\multirow{7}{*}{\makecell[l]{Genetically determined\\stone formation}} & Cystinuria (type A, B and AB) & 0 & 0 \\
& Primary hyperoxaluria (PH) & 0 & 0 \\
& Renal tubular acidosis (RTA) type I & 0 & 0 \\
& 2,8-Dihydroxyadeninuria & 0 & 0 \\
& Xanthinuria & 0 & 0 \\
& Lesch-Nyhan syndrome & 0 & 0 \\
& Cystic fibrosis & 1 (0.1\%) & 38 (0.3\%) \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{Drug-induced stone formation} & & \\
\midrule
\multirow{6}{*}{\makecell[l]{Anatomical abnormalities\\associated with stone\\formation}} & Medullary sponge kidney (tubular ectasia) & 12 (0.1\%) & 20 (0.2\%) \\
& Ureteropelvic junction (UPJ) obstruction & 98 (1.1\%) & 47 (0.4\%) \\
& Calyceal diverticulum, calyceal cyst & 0 & 0 \\
& Vesico-uretero-renal reflux & 9 (0.1\%) & 29 (0.3\%) \\
& Horseshoe kidney & 2 (0.1\%) & 0 \\
& Ureterocele & 6 (0.1\%) & 0 \\
\midrule
\multirow{2}{*}{\makecell[l]{Environmental and\\professional factors}} & High ambient temperatures & Not available & Not available \\
& Chronic lead and cadmium exposure & Not available & Not available \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}
\end{landscape}

\newpage

## Supplementary Table 2. Analyses Performed

Explanatory table of additional sensitivity analyses and outcome analyses performed at each starting point. *X* indicates the analysis was performed; *N/A* means the analysis is not applicable to that starting point. The "Short time" between events and "recurrent disease" definitions only apply from 2nd event onwards.For the "recurrent disease" the prior events includes the current stone event.

\begin{table}[H]
\centering
\small
\begin{tabular}{p{1.5cm}|p{2.4cm}|p{2.4cm}p{1.9cm}|ccc|p{4.6cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{\makecell[l]{Analysis\\type}}
& \textbf{Starting point}
& \multicolumn{2}{c|}{\textbf{Definition Exploration}}
& \multicolumn{3}{c|}{\textbf{Outcome Analyses}}
& \textbf{Outcome description} \\
&
& \makecell{`Short time'\\definition} & \makecell{`Recurrent'\\disease\\definition}
& \textbf{KM} & \textbf{DCA} & \textbf{ROC} & \\
\midrule
\multirow{4}{*}{Per event}
& 1\textsuperscript{st} event
& N/A & N/A
& X & X & X
& \multirow{5}{=}{\textbf{KM:} 1\textsuperscript{st} \textbf{subsequent} event (time-to-event, all included).\\[4pt]\textbf{DCA + ROC:} 1, 2, 3 \textbf{subsequent} events (binary; censored <5y removed).} \\
\cmidrule(lr){2-7}
& 2\textsuperscript{nd} event
& \multirow{4}{=}{\centering 6 → 24 mo, 3-mo increments}
& \multirow{4}{=}{\centering 2, 3, 4 \textbf{prior} events}
& X & X & X & \\
\cmidrule(lr){2-2}\cmidrule(lr){5-7}
& 3\textsuperscript{rd} event
& & & X & X & X & \\
\cmidrule(lr){2-2}\cmidrule(lr){5-7}
& 4\textsuperscript{th} event
& & & X & X & X & \\
\cmidrule(lr){1-2}\cmidrule(lr){5-7}
Any event
& \makecell[l]{Any stone\\presentation\\(1\textsuperscript{st} → 4\textsuperscript{th})}
& & & X & X & X & \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\newpage

## Supplementary Table 3. Inter-event time by transition

Descriptive statistics of the time between consecutive stone events at each transition, derived from Kaplan Meier plots of the entire transitions dataset. The attenuation analyses (Sup Figs 39–41) show that the *count* of prior events discriminates better than any binary cut-off on "short-time" between stone events.N = number of participants who experienced the *subsequent* event of each transition (e.g. second event after first event). The "Within X" rows are the cumulative proportion of patients (who recurred) whose next event occurred within the stated window.

```{r supp-table-3-inter-event, include=FALSE}
# Robust loader: if either CSV is missing, use an empty data frame with the
# expected schema so the inline `r ...` calls below still render "--" rather
# than blowing up the knit.
.iet_schema <- data.frame(
  transition = character(0), n = integer(0),
  mean_months = numeric(0), sd_months = numeric(0),
  median_months = numeric(0), q1_months = numeric(0), q3_months = numeric(0),
  pct_within_6mo = numeric(0), pct_within_1yr = numeric(0),
  pct_within_2yr = numeric(0), pct_within_5yr = numeric(0)
)
load_iet <- function(path) {
  if (file.exists(path)) {
    read.csv(path, check.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    message("Note: ", path, " not found — Sup Table 3 will render placeholders.")
    .iet_schema
  }
}
ukb_iet <- load_iet("UKB Recurrence paper results/inter_event_time_summary.csv")
aou_iet <- load_iet("AoU Recurrence paper results/inter_event_time_summary_aou.csv")

fmt_mean <- function(df, tr) {
  r <- df[df$transition == tr, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(r) == 0) return("--")
  sprintf("%.1f $\\pm$ %.1f", r$mean_months, r$sd_months)
}
fmt_med <- function(df, tr) {
  r <- df[df$transition == tr, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(r) == 0) return("--")
  sprintf("%.1f (%.1f--%.1f)", r$median_months, r$q1_months, r$q3_months)
}
fmt_pct <- function(df, tr, col) {
  r <- df[df$transition == tr, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(r) == 0) return("--")
  sprintf("%.1f\\%%", r[[col]])
}
fmt_n <- function(df, tr) {
  r <- df[df$transition == tr, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(r) == 0) return("--")
  format(r$n, big.mark = ",")
}
```

\begin{table}[H]
\centering
\small
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
\textbf{Transition} & \textbf{1$\rightarrow$2} & \textbf{2$\rightarrow$3} & \textbf{3$\rightarrow$4} & \textbf{4$\rightarrow$5} \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{UK Biobank}} \\
\midrule
N (recurred) & `r fmt_n(ukb_iet, "1→2")` & `r fmt_n(ukb_iet, "2→3")` & `r fmt_n(ukb_iet, "3→4")` & `r fmt_n(ukb_iet, "4→5")` \\
Median (IQR) (months) & `r fmt_med(ukb_iet, "1→2")` & `r fmt_med(ukb_iet, "2→3")` & `r fmt_med(ukb_iet, "3→4")` & `r fmt_med(ukb_iet, "4→5")` \\
Within 1 yr   & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "1→2", "pct_within_1yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "2→3", "pct_within_1yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "3→4", "pct_within_1yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "4→5", "pct_within_1yr")` \\
Within 2 yr   & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "1→2", "pct_within_2yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "2→3", "pct_within_2yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "3→4", "pct_within_2yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "4→5", "pct_within_2yr")` \\
Within 5 yr   & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "1→2", "pct_within_5yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "2→3", "pct_within_5yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "3→4", "pct_within_5yr")` & `r fmt_pct(ukb_iet, "4→5", "pct_within_5yr")` \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{All of Us}} \\
\midrule
N (recurred) & `r fmt_n(aou_iet, "1→2")` & `r fmt_n(aou_iet, "2→3")` & `r fmt_n(aou_iet, "3→4")` & `r fmt_n(aou_iet, "4→5")` \\
Median (IQR) (months) & `r fmt_med(aou_iet, "1→2")` & `r fmt_med(aou_iet, "2→3")` & `r fmt_med(aou_iet, "3→4")` & `r fmt_med(aou_iet, "4→5")` \\
Within 1 yr   & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "1→2", "pct_within_1yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "2→3", "pct_within_1yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "3→4", "pct_within_1yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "4→5", "pct_within_1yr")` \\
Within 2 yr   & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "1→2", "pct_within_2yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "2→3", "pct_within_2yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "3→4", "pct_within_2yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "4→5", "pct_within_2yr")` \\
Within 5 yr   & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "1→2", "pct_within_5yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "2→3", "pct_within_5yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "3→4", "pct_within_5yr")` & `r fmt_pct(aou_iet, "4→5", "pct_within_5yr")` \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\newpage

## Supplementary Table 4. Pairwise classifier comparisons (whole-dataset analysis, Figure 2)

Pairwise tests were computed within each cohort on the entire transitions-long sample using a non-parametric cluster bootstrap that resamples PARTICIPANTS (not transitions) — addressing the within-patient correlation introduced when one participant contributes multiple transition-rows. Cluster-bootstrap two-sided p-values are then combined across cohorts via Fisher's method. Pooled AUCs use DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis (UKB + AoU); between-cohort heterogeneity for each classifier (Cochran's $Q$, $I^2$, $\tau^2$, $p_Q$) is reported separately in **Supplementary Table 5**. The six comparison families correspond to the analysis in main-text Figure 2.

```{r supp-table-4-pairwise-comparisons, results='asis'}
# --- Load pooled meta-AUC table (used for Figure 2) and pairwise DeLong tables ---
load_or_empty_pair <- function(path) {
  if (file.exists(path)) {
    read.csv(path, check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  } else {
    message("Note: ", path, " not found — Sup Table 4 will report 'NA'.")
    data.frame(classifier_a = character(0), classifier_b = character(0),
               delong_p = numeric(0))
  }
}
ukb_pair <- load_or_empty_pair("UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_pairwise_delong.csv")
aou_pair <- load_or_empty_pair("AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_pairwise_delong_aou.csv")

# Per-cohort AUCs (already loaded by Figure 2 chunk; reload defensively here
# so this chunk works even if knitted in isolation)
load_or_empty_metrics <- function(path) {
  if (file.exists(path)) {
    read.csv(path, check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  } else {
    message("Note: ", path, " not found.")
    data.frame(Comparison = character(0), AUC_val = numeric(0),
               AUC_lo = numeric(0), AUC_hi = numeric(0))
  }
}
ukb_m <- load_or_empty_metrics("UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_metrics.csv")
aou_m <- load_or_empty_metrics("AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_metrics_aou.csv")

# DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis of AUC for a given classifier,
# with between-cohort heterogeneity statistics (Cochran's Q, I², τ², df, p_Q).
# Cohort-specific AUCs come from the cluster-bootstrap CIs in the per-cohort
# whole-dataset metrics CSVs.
ivm_auc <- function(label) {
  rows <- rbind(
    ukb_m[ukb_m$Comparison == label, c("AUC_val","AUC_lo","AUC_hi"), drop = FALSE],
    aou_m[aou_m$Comparison == label, c("AUC_val","AUC_lo","AUC_hi"), drop = FALSE]
  )
  na_out <- c(est = NA_real_, lo = NA_real_, hi = NA_real_,
              Q = NA_real_, df = NA_real_, I2 = NA_real_, tau2 = NA_real_,
              p_Q = NA_real_, k = NA_real_)
  if (nrow(rows) == 0) return(na_out)
  se <- (rows$AUC_hi - rows$AUC_lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  ok <- is.finite(se) & se > 0 & is.finite(rows$AUC_val)
  est <- rows$AUC_val[ok]; se <- se[ok]; vi <- se^2
  k <- length(est)
  if (k == 0) return(na_out)
  if (k == 1) return(c(est = est, lo = est - 1.96*se, hi = est + 1.96*se,
                       Q = NA_real_, df = NA_real_, I2 = NA_real_,
                       tau2 = NA_real_, p_Q = NA_real_, k = 1))
  w_fe  <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * est) / sum(w_fe)
  Q     <- sum(w_fe * (est - fe_pe)^2)
  df_Q  <- k - 1
  C     <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2  <- max(0, (Q - df_Q) / C)
  I2    <- max(0, (Q - df_Q) / Q) * 100   # Higgins & Thompson (2002)
  p_Q   <- 1 - pchisq(Q, df = df_Q)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * est) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(est = pe, lo = pe - 1.96 * pse, hi = pe + 1.96 * pse,
    Q = Q, df = df_Q, I2 = I2, tau2 = tau2, p_Q = p_Q, k = k)
}

# Look up the DeLong p in a pairwise table (handles either order of a/b)
lookup_p <- function(tbl, a, b) {
  hit <- tbl[(tbl$classifier_a == a & tbl$classifier_b == b) |
             (tbl$classifier_a == b & tbl$classifier_b == a), "delong_p"]
  if (length(hit) == 0) NA_real_ else hit[1]
}

fisher_combine <- function(ps) {
  ps <- ps[is.finite(ps) & ps > 0 & ps <= 1]
  if (length(ps) < 1) return(NA_real_)
  if (length(ps) == 1) return(ps)
  X <- -2 * sum(log(ps))
  1 - pchisq(X, df = 2 * length(ps))
}

fmt_auc_ci <- function(v) {
  # Only check the three fields we actually use; ivm_auc() now also returns
  # heterogeneity statistics (Q, df, I², τ², p_Q, k) which can legitimately
  # be NA for single-cohort cases without invalidating the est/lo/hi triple.
  if (any(is.na(c(v["est"], v["lo"], v["hi"])))) return("--")
  sprintf("%.3f (%.3f--%.3f)", v["est"], v["lo"], v["hi"])
}
fmt_p_combined <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("--")
  if (p < 0.001) return("<0.001")
  sprintf("%.3f", p)
}

# --- Define the 5 comparison families ---
all_classifiers <- c(
  "EAU only",
  "EAU + Short Time ≤6mo", "EAU + Short Time ≤24mo",
  "EAU + ≥2 events", "EAU + ≥3 events", "EAU + ≥4 events",
  "EAU + ≥2 events + Short Time ≤6mo",
  "EAU + ≥2 events + Short Time ≤24mo",
  "EAU + ≥3 events + Short Time ≤6mo",
  "EAU + ≥3 events + Short Time ≤24mo",
  "EAU + ≥4 events + Short Time ≤6mo",
  "EAU + ≥4 events + Short Time ≤24mo"
)
ST    <- c("EAU + Short Time ≤6mo", "EAU + Short Time ≤24mo")
RD    <- c("EAU + ≥2 events", "EAU + ≥3 events", "EAU + ≥4 events")
RDST  <- setdiff(all_classifiers, c("EAU only", ST, RD))

family_specs <- list(
  list(name = "1. EAU only vs EAU + Short Time (no recurrent disease)",
       a = rep("EAU only", length(ST)), b = ST),
  list(name = "2. EAU only vs EAU + Recurrent Disease (no short time)",
       a = rep("EAU only", length(RD)), b = RD),
  list(name = "3. EAU only vs EAU + Recurrent Disease + Short Time",
       a = rep("EAU only", length(RDST)), b = RDST),
  list(name = "4. EAU + Short Time vs EAU + Recurrent Disease (no short time)",
       a = rep(ST, each = length(RD)), b = rep(RD, length(ST))),
  list(name = "5. EAU + Short Time vs EAU + Recurrent Disease + Short Time",
       a = rep(ST, each = length(RDST)), b = rep(RDST, length(ST))),
  list(name = "6. EAU + Recurrent Disease (no short time) vs EAU + Recurrent Disease + Short Time",
       a = rep(RD, each = length(RDST)), b = rep(RDST, length(RD)))
)

rows_list <- lapply(family_specs, function(fs) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(fs$a), function(i) {
    a <- fs$a[i]; b <- fs$b[i]
    p_ukb <- lookup_p(ukb_pair, a, b)
    p_aou <- lookup_p(aou_pair, a, b)
    p_pool <- fisher_combine(c(p_ukb, p_aou))
    auc_a <- ivm_auc(a); auc_b <- ivm_auc(b)
    data.frame(
      Family = fs$name,
      A = a, B = b,
      `AUC A pooled (95% CI)` = fmt_auc_ci(auc_a),
      `AUC B pooled (95% CI)` = fmt_auc_ci(auc_b),
      `UKB cluster-boot p` = fmt_p_combined(p_ukb),
      `AoU cluster-boot p` = fmt_p_combined(p_aou),
      `Fisher combined p` = fmt_p_combined(p_pool),
      check.names = FALSE,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
})
all_rows <- do.call(rbind, rows_list)

# --- Render via kableExtra with family group headers ---
suppressMessages({ library(kableExtra) })
display <- all_rows %>% dplyr::select(-Family)
group_index <- table(factor(all_rows$Family, levels = sapply(family_specs, `[[`, "name")))

kbl(display, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
    longtable = TRUE,
    col.names = c("High Risk Definition A", "High Risk Definition B",
                  "AUC A (95\\% CI)", "AUC B (95\\% CI)",
                  "UKB cluster-boot $p$",
                  "AoU cluster-boot $p$",
                  "Fisher combined $p$")) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("repeat_header", "hold_position"),
                font_size = 7) %>%
  pack_rows(index = group_index) %>%
  # Constrain all column widths so the 7-col longtable fits inside the
  # 16.5-cm text width without truncating the right-most (Fisher combined)
  # column.
  column_spec(1, width = "3.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "3.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "2.0cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "2.0cm") %>%
  column_spec(5, width = "1.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(6, width = "1.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(7, width = "1.5cm") %>%
  # Vertically merge consecutive duplicate values in column 1 (High Risk
  # Definition A) and column 3 (AUC A). pack_rows boundaries naturally
  # break the merge, so collapsing happens within each family block only.
  collapse_rows(columns = c(1, 3), valign = "middle", latex_hline = "none")
```

\newpage

## Supplementary Table 5. Between-cohort heterogeneity for the whole-dataset pooled AUCs

Heterogeneity statistics to accompany Figure 2.

\begin{landscape}

```{r supp-table-5-heterogeneity, results='asis'}
# Reuses ukb_m, aou_m, ivm_auc(), all_classifiers from the Sup Table 4 chunk.
# ivm_auc() now returns Q, df, I2, tau2, p_Q in addition to est/lo/hi.
fmt_p_q <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("--")
  if (p < 0.001) return("<0.001")
  sprintf("%.3f", p)
}
fmt_q   <- function(q)  if (is.na(q))  "--" else sprintf("%.2f", q)
fmt_i2  <- function(i)  if (is.na(i))  "--" else sprintf("%.1f\\%%", i)
fmt_t2  <- function(t)  if (is.na(t))  "--" else sprintf("%.2e", t)
fmt_per_cohort <- function(metrics_df, label) {
  r <- metrics_df[metrics_df$Comparison == label, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(r) == 0) return("--")
  sprintf("%.3f (%.3f--%.3f)",
          as.numeric(r$AUC_val[1]),
          as.numeric(r$AUC_lo[1]),
          as.numeric(r$AUC_hi[1]))
}

het_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(all_classifiers, function(lbl) {
  v <- ivm_auc(lbl)
  data.frame(
    Classifier      = lbl,
    `UKB AUC (95% CI)`    = fmt_per_cohort(ukb_m, lbl),
    `AoU AUC (95% CI)`    = fmt_per_cohort(aou_m, lbl),
    `Pooled AUC (95% CI)` = fmt_auc_ci(v),
    Q       = fmt_q(unname(v["Q"])),
    df      = if (is.na(v["df"])) "--" else as.character(as.integer(v["df"])),
    `I^2`   = fmt_i2(unname(v["I2"])),
    `tau^2` = fmt_t2(unname(v["tau2"])),
    `p_Q`   = fmt_p_q(unname(v["p_Q"])),
    check.names = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

kbl(het_rows, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
    longtable = FALSE,                       # landscape page → single table OK
    align = c("l", rep("c", 8)),
    col.names = c("Classifier",
                  "UKB AUC (95\\% CI)", "AoU AUC (95\\% CI)",
                  "Pooled AUC (95\\% CI)",
                  "$Q$", "df", "$I^2$", "$\\tau^2$", "$p_Q$")) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"),
                font_size = 8,
                position = "center") %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "5.0cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "2.6cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "2.6cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "2.6cm") %>%
  column_spec(5, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(6, width = "0.7cm") %>%
  column_spec(7, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(8, width = "1.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(9, width = "1.2cm")
```

\end{landscape}


\newpage

# Figures

## Figure 1
```{r fig1-forest-plot-data, fig.width=10, fig.height=14, out.width="100%"}
# --- Load data ---
ukb <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/prognostic_accuracy_grid_search_results.csv")
aou <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/prognostic_accuracy_grid_search_results_aou.csv")

# Remove duplicate rows in AoU
aou <- aou[!duplicated(aou[, c("Starting_Point", "Time_Cutoff_Months", "Recurrence_Definition")]), ]

# --- Build labels ---
event_map <- c("Index Date"   = "First Event",
               "Second Event" = "Second Event",
               "Third Event"  = "Third Event",
               "Fourth Event" = "Fourth Event")

make_label <- function(starting_point, cutoff) {
  event <- event_map[starting_point]
  if (is.na(cutoff) | cutoff == "NA") {
    event
  } else {
    paste0(event, " (", cutoff, "mo)")
  }
}

# --- Prepare UKB ---
ukb$Biobank <- "UK Biobank"
ukb$label <- mapply(make_label, ukb$Starting_Point,
                    as.character(ukb$Time_Cutoff_Months))

# --- Prepare AoU ---
aou$Biobank <- "All of Us"
aou$label <- mapply(make_label, aou$Starting_Point,
                    as.character(aou$Time_Cutoff_Months))

# --- Combine ---
cols <- c("label", "Starting_Point", "Time_Cutoff_Months", "Recurrence_Definition",
          "AUC_val", "AUC_lo", "AUC_hi", "Biobank")
df <- rbind(ukb[, cols], aou[, cols])

# Ensure numeric
df$AUC_val <- as.numeric(df$AUC_val)
df$AUC_lo <- as.numeric(df$AUC_lo)
df$AUC_hi <- as.numeric(df$AUC_hi)

# --- DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis on AUC ---
# SE derived from 95% CI bounds (normal approximation). With only 2 cohorts
# the between-study variance estimate (tau^2) is unstable, but RE pooling
# is the methodologically appropriate choice when between-cohort
# heterogeneity is plausible. tau^2 = 0 collapses to fixed-effect IVM.
re_meta_auc <- function(est, lo, hi) {
  se <- (hi - lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  ok <- is.finite(se) & se > 0 & is.finite(est)
  est <- est[ok]; se <- se[ok]; vi <- se^2
  k <- length(est)
  if (k < 1) return(c(est = NA_real_, lo = NA_real_, hi = NA_real_))
  if (k == 1) return(c(est = est, lo = est - 1.96*se, hi = est + 1.96*se))
  w_fe <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * est) / sum(w_fe)
  Q  <- sum(w_fe * (est - fe_pe)^2)
  C  <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2 <- max(0, (Q - (k - 1)) / C)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * est) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(est = pe, lo = pe - 1.96 * pse, hi = pe + 1.96 * pse)
}

meta_auc <- df %>%
  mutate(SE = (AUC_hi - AUC_lo) / (2 * 1.96)) %>%
  filter(is.finite(SE) & SE > 0) %>%
  group_by(label, Starting_Point, Time_Cutoff_Months, Recurrence_Definition) %>%
  summarise(
    pe = list(re_meta_auc(AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    AUC_val = sapply(pe, `[`, "est"),
    AUC_lo  = sapply(pe, `[`, "lo"),
    AUC_hi  = sapply(pe, `[`, "hi"),
    Biobank = "Meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(cols))

df <- rbind(df, meta_auc)

# --- Order labels by event then cutoff ---
event_order <- c("First Event", "Second Event", "Third Event", "Fourth Event")
df$event_label <- event_map[df$Starting_Point]
df$event_order <- match(df$event_label, event_order)
df$cutoff_num <- ifelse(is.na(df$Time_Cutoff_Months), 0,
                        as.numeric(df$Time_Cutoff_Months))

df <- df[order(df$event_order, df$cutoff_num), ]

# --- Y-axis: cutoff-only labels (event is in the row facet strip) ---
# For Index Date and the no-cutoff variant of 2nd/3rd/4th events we use a
# single shared label "No Short time" — there is no short-time modifier in
# either case (Index Date has no prior gap; the no-cutoff non-Index variant
# explicitly omits the short-time rule). The 6- and 24-month short-time rows
# sit below it in each non-Index facet.
df$cutoff_label <- dplyr::case_when(
  df$Starting_Point == "Index Date"                                ~ "No Short time",
  is.na(df$Time_Cutoff_Months) | df$Time_Cutoff_Months == "NA"     ~ "No Short time",
  TRUE                                                             ~ paste0(df$Time_Cutoff_Months, " months")
)
# ---  Order cutoff labels (top→bottom: No Short time, 6 months, 24 months) ---
# In ggplot, factor level 1 is at the bottom. To put "No Short time"
# on top we place it last in the factor levels.
cutoff_levels <- c("24 months", "6 months", "No Short time")
df$cutoff_label <- factor(df$cutoff_label, levels = cutoff_levels)

# ---  Condense information ---
df <- df %>%
  filter(Recurrence_Definition == "≥1 subsequent stone events") %>%
  filter(
    Starting_Point == "Index Date" |
    (Starting_Point != "Index Date" &
       (is.na(Time_Cutoff_Months) | Time_Cutoff_Months %in% c(6, 24)))
  )

# --- Cohort factor order: UKB, AoU, Meta-analysis ---
df$Biobank <- factor(df$Biobank,
                     levels = c("UK Biobank", "All of Us", "Meta-analysis"))

# --- Row facet: event group ---
df$event_label <- factor(df$event_label, levels = event_order)

# --- Column facet: recurrence definition ---
rec_labels <- c("\u22651 subsequent stone events" = "\u2265 1 Subsequent Stone Events")
df$rec_facet <- rec_labels[df$Recurrence_Definition]
df$rec_facet <- factor(df$rec_facet, levels = rec_labels)

# --- Manual dodge offset (so 3 groups stay aligned regardless of layer) ---
dodge_w <- 0.7
df$y_offset <- dplyr::case_when(
  df$Biobank == "UK Biobank"    ~ +dodge_w / 3,
  df$Biobank == "All of Us"     ~  0,
  df$Biobank == "Meta-analysis" ~ -dodge_w / 3
)
df$y_num <- as.numeric(df$cutoff_label) + df$y_offset

# --- Ensure ONLY desired cutoff levels exist ---
df <- df %>% dplyr::filter(cutoff_num %in% c(0, 6, 24))
df$cutoff_label <- factor(df$cutoff_label, levels = cutoff_levels)

# --- Studies + meta-analysis split ---
df_studies <- df %>% dplyr::filter(Biobank != "Meta-analysis")
df_meta    <- df %>% dplyr::filter(Biobank == "Meta-analysis")

# --- Pairwise Wald-test p-values on pooled meta-analytic AUCs ---
# For each non-Index event (Second/Third/Fourth) we compute three comparisons:
#   (i)   No Short time  vs 6 months
#   (ii)  No Short time  vs 24 months
#   (iii) 6 months        vs 24 months
# Each p uses z = (AUC_a - AUC_b) / sqrt(SE_a^2 + SE_b^2), where SE comes
# from the pooled meta-analytic CI: SE = (AUC_hi - AUC_lo) / (2 * 1.96).
# Treats the pooled estimates as independent (conservative — paired
# discrimination tests on the same subjects have negative covariance that
# would shrink the SE of the difference).
fmt_pp <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 0.001) return("<0.001")
  formatC(p, digits = 3, format = "f")
}
wald_p <- function(a, b) {
  if (nrow(a) == 0 || nrow(b) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  se_a <- (a$AUC_hi - a$AUC_lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  se_b <- (b$AUC_hi - b$AUC_lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  z    <- (a$AUC_val - b$AUC_val) / sqrt(se_a^2 + se_b^2)
  2 * pnorm(-abs(z))
}

pair_events <- df_meta %>%
  dplyr::filter(Starting_Point != "Index Date") %>%
  dplyr::distinct(event_label) %>%
  dplyr::pull(event_label)

pval_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(pair_events, function(ev) {
  rows  <- df_meta %>% dplyr::filter(event_label == ev)
  nst   <- rows %>% dplyr::filter(cutoff_label == "No Short time")
  six   <- rows %>% dplyr::filter(cutoff_label == "6 months")
  twf   <- rows %>% dplyr::filter(cutoff_label == "24 months")
  if (nrow(nst) == 0 && nrow(six) == 0 && nrow(twf) == 0) return(NULL)

  p1 <- wald_p(nst, six)   # No Short time vs 6 mo
  p2 <- wald_p(nst, twf)   # No Short time vs 24 mo
  p3 <- wald_p(six, twf)   # 6 mo vs 24 mo

  # Inset-table label content: header, separator and three pairwise rows,
  # rendered in a monospaced font so the columns align inside the box.
  col1 <- 23  # width of the "Comparison" column (chars)
  col2 <- 7   # width of the "p" column (chars)
  table_text <- paste(
    sprintf(paste0("%-", col1, "s  %-", col2, "s"), "Comparison",        "p-value"),
    sprintf(paste0("%-", col1, "s  %-", col2, "s"),
            strrep("-", col1), strrep("-", col2)),
    sprintf(paste0("%-", col1, "s  %-", col2, "s"), "No Short time vs 6 mo",  fmt_pp(p1)),
    sprintf(paste0("%-", col1, "s  %-", col2, "s"), "No Short time vs 24 mo", fmt_pp(p2)),
    sprintf(paste0("%-", col1, "s  %-", col2, "s"), "6 mo vs 24 mo",          fmt_pp(p3)),
    sep = "\n"
  )

  # Place block centred on the middle row ("6 months") of this event,
  # just to the right of the diamond region.
  y_anchor <- as.numeric(factor("6 months", levels = cutoff_levels)) -
                (dodge_w / 3)

  data.frame(event_label = as.character(ev),
             rec_facet   = unique(rows$rec_facet),
             x_text      = 0.72,
             y_num       = y_anchor,
             p_label     = table_text,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}))

if (is.null(pval_df) || nrow(pval_df) == 0) {
  pval_df <- data.frame(event_label = factor(character(0), levels = event_order),
                        rec_facet   = factor(character(0), levels = rec_labels),
                        x_text = numeric(0), y_num = numeric(0),
                        p_label = character(0))
} else {
  pval_df$event_label <- factor(pval_df$event_label, levels = event_order)
}

# --- Manual dodge offsets ---
dodge_w <- 0.7
df_studies$y_num <- as.numeric(df_studies$cutoff_label) +
  dplyr::case_when(
    df_studies$Biobank == "UK Biobank" ~ +dodge_w / 3,
    df_studies$Biobank == "All of Us"  ~  0
  )
df_meta$y_num <- as.numeric(df_meta$cutoff_label) - (dodge_w / 3)

# --- Diamond polygon vertices ---
diamond_h <- 0.13
diamond_df <- df_meta %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::do(data.frame(
    label       = .$label,
    event_label = .$event_label,
    rec_facet   = .$rec_facet,
    Biobank     = "Meta-analysis",
    x = c(.$AUC_lo, .$AUC_val, .$AUC_hi, .$AUC_val),
    y = .$y_num + c(0, diamond_h, 0, -diamond_h)
  )) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# --- Axis breaks ---
y_breaks <- seq_along(levels(df$cutoff_label))
y_labels <- levels(df$cutoff_label)

# --- Plot ---
ggplot() +

  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = 0.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#F4A3A3", alpha = 0.2) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 0.7, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#F6D186", alpha = 0.2) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.7, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#A6D96A", alpha = 0.2) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.7, linetype = "dotdash", colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.7) +

  geom_errorbarh(data = df_studies,
                 aes(xmin = AUC_lo, xmax = AUC_hi, y = y_num, colour = Biobank),
                 height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.9) +

  geom_point(data = df_studies,
             aes(x = AUC_val, y = y_num, colour = Biobank, shape = Biobank),
             size = 3.5, stroke = 0.6) +

  geom_polygon(data = diamond_df,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = label, fill = Biobank),
               colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +

  geom_label(data = pval_df,
             aes(x = x_text, y = y_num, label = p_label),
             hjust = 0, vjust = 0.5,
             size = 2.4, lineheight = 0.95,
             family = "mono", colour = "black",
             fill = "white",
             label.size = 0.3,        
             label.padding = unit(3, "pt"),
             label.r = unit(1, "pt")) +

  facet_grid(event_label ~ rec_facet,
             scales = "free_y", space = "free_y", switch = "y") +

  scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks,
                     labels = y_labels,
                     expand = expansion(add = 0.6)) +

  scale_colour_manual(values = c("UK Biobank" = "#1b9e77",
                                 "All of Us"  = "#d95f02")) +

  scale_shape_manual(values = c("UK Biobank" = 16,
                                "All of Us"  = 17)) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Meta-analysis" = "#7570b3"),
                    labels = "Meta-analysis") +

  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.45, 0.8)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.45, 0.75, 0.05)) +

  labs(x = "Area Under Receiver-Operator-Characteristic Curve (95% CI)", y = "Short Time Between Events, months",
       title = "EAU Risk Stratification by Event Presentation",
       subtitle = "Forest Plot of AUC values for Prediction of Subsequent Symptomatic Event in 5 years. Split by Number of Events at Presentation") +

  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", size = 9),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95"),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    panel.spacing = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  )
```

**Figure 1:** Forest plot of area under the receiver-operator-characteristic curve (AUC-ROC) for the Baseline EAU risk stratification's (no 'recurrent disease' definition) predictive ability for 5-year recurrence, stratified by stone event number on the left. The y-axis denotes the *Short Time Between Events*. The "No Short time" row reports the EAU classifier without any short-time modifier; it appears at the top of every event facet (for the 1st event there is no prior gap so the modifier is not applicable, and for the 2nd/3rd/4th events it serves as a reference against which the short-time variants can be compared). Dashed line indicates AUC = 0.5 i.e. no difference between high/low risk, Dot/Dash line indicates AUC = 0.7 (generally accepted threshold for meaningful clinical difference). Green circle = UK Biobank, Orange triangle = All of Us, Purple diamond = DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis of UKB + AoU. The diamond's horizontal extent indicates the pooled 95% CI. The boxed inset table to the right of each non-Index-Date event facet reports three pairwise Wald-test *p*-values comparing the pooled meta-analytic AUCs of (i) No Short time vs 6-month short-time, (ii) No Short time vs 24-month short-time, and (iii) 6-month vs 24-month short-time. Test treats the pooled AUCs as independent (conservative; see methods).

\newpage

## Figure 2

```{r fig2-eau-vs-recurrent-disease, fig.width=11, fig.height=11, out.width="100%"}
# --- Load per-cohort whole-dataset analysis results ---
# These come from the whole-dataset analysis (every participant contributes
# one row per transition they reach, using per-event re-ascertained EAU risk),
# saved by `prognostic_accuracy_grid_search.Rmd` and `symptomatic_occurences.ipynb`.
ukb_p <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_metrics.csv",
                  check.names = FALSE)
aou_p <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_metrics_aou.csv",
                  check.names = FALSE)

# Relabel each classifier definition. We detect three orthogonal features
# in each Comparison string:
#   - rec_def: "\u22652", "\u22653", "\u22654", or "none"
#   - st_cut : 6, 24, or NA (no short-time modifier)
# and combine them into a Group label and a Risk_Family for facetting.
classify_row <- function(rd) {
  has_2  <- grepl("\u22652 events", rd)
  has_3  <- grepl("\u22653 events", rd)
  has_4  <- grepl("\u22654 events", rd)
  has_st6  <- grepl("Short Time \u22646mo|Short Time <=6mo|Short Time \\u22646mo", rd)
  has_st24 <- grepl("Short Time \u226424mo|Short Time <=24mo|Short Time \\u226424mo", rd)
  rec <- dplyr::case_when(
    has_2 ~ "\u22652 events",
    has_3 ~ "\u22653 events",
    has_4 ~ "\u22654 events",
    TRUE  ~ "none"
  )
  st <- dplyr::case_when(
    has_st6  ~ "Short Time \u22646mo",
    has_st24 ~ "Short Time \u226424mo",
    TRUE     ~ "no short time"
  )
  list(rec = rec, st = st)
}
classified_ukb <- lapply(ukb_p$Comparison, classify_row)
ukb_p$rec_def <- vapply(classified_ukb, function(x) x$rec, character(1))
ukb_p$st_def  <- vapply(classified_ukb, function(x) x$st,  character(1))
classified_aou <- lapply(aou_p$Comparison, classify_row)
aou_p$rec_def <- vapply(classified_aou, function(x) x$rec, character(1))
aou_p$st_def  <- vapply(classified_aou, function(x) x$st,  character(1))

# Build human-readable Group label (per-row) and a Risk_Family (facet)
make_group <- function(rec, st) {
  if (rec == "none" && st == "no short time") return("EAU only")
  base <- if (rec == "none") "EAU" else paste0("EAU + ", rec)
  if (st == "no short time") base else paste0(base, " + ", st)
}
make_family <- function(rec, st) {
  if (rec == "none" && st == "no short time") return("EAU only (baseline)")
  if (rec == "none") return("EAU + Short Time")
  paste0("EAU + ", rec)
}

ukb_p$Group        <- mapply(make_group,  ukb_p$rec_def, ukb_p$st_def)
ukb_p$Risk_Family  <- mapply(make_family, ukb_p$rec_def, ukb_p$st_def)
aou_p$Group        <- mapply(make_group,  aou_p$rec_def, aou_p$st_def)
aou_p$Risk_Family  <- mapply(make_family, aou_p$rec_def, aou_p$st_def)
ukb_p$Cohort <- "UKB"; aou_p$Cohort <- "AoU"

# Standardise the DeLong p-value column name across the two CSVs
ukb_p$delong_p <- ukb_p$delong_p_vs_EAU_only
aou_p$delong_p <- aou_p$delong_p_vs_EAU_only

studies <- rbind(
  ukb_p[, c("Group","Risk_Family","AUC_val","AUC_lo","AUC_hi","delong_p","Cohort")],
  aou_p[, c("Group","Risk_Family","AUC_val","AUC_lo","AUC_hi","delong_p","Cohort")]
)

group_levels <- c(
  "EAU only",
  "EAU + Short Time \u22646mo",
  "EAU + Short Time \u226424mo",
  "EAU + \u22652 events",
  "EAU + \u22652 events + Short Time \u22646mo",
  "EAU + \u22652 events + Short Time \u226424mo",
  "EAU + \u22653 events",
  "EAU + \u22653 events + Short Time \u22646mo",
  "EAU + \u22653 events + Short Time \u226424mo",
  "EAU + \u22654 events",
  "EAU + \u22654 events + Short Time \u22646mo",
  "EAU + \u22654 events + Short Time \u226424mo"
)
family_levels <- c("EAU only (baseline)", "EAU + Short Time",
                   "EAU + \u22652 events", "EAU + \u22653 events", "EAU + \u22654 events")

studies$Group       <- factor(studies$Group,       levels = group_levels)
studies$Risk_Family <- factor(studies$Risk_Family, levels = family_levels)

# --- DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis on AUC ---
# With only 2 cohorts the between-study variance estimate (tau^2) is
# unstable, but the user explicitly requested random-effects pooling so
# between-cohort heterogeneity is acknowledged in the SE. When tau^2 = 0
# (no detectable heterogeneity) the pool collapses to fixed-effect IVM.
re_meta <- function(est, lo, hi) {
  se <- (hi - lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  ok <- is.finite(se) & se > 0 & is.finite(est)
  est <- est[ok]; se <- se[ok]; vi <- se^2
  k <- length(est)
  if (k < 1) return(c(est = NA_real_, lo = NA_real_, hi = NA_real_, tau2 = NA_real_))
  if (k == 1) return(c(est = est, lo = est - 1.96*se, hi = est + 1.96*se, tau2 = 0))
  w_fe <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * est) / sum(w_fe)
  Q  <- sum(w_fe * (est - fe_pe)^2)
  C  <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2 <- max(0, (Q - (k - 1)) / C)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * est) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(est = pe, lo = pe - 1.96 * pse, hi = pe + 1.96 * pse, tau2 = tau2)
}

# --- Fisher's combined p-value across cohorts (treats two as independent tests) ---
fisher_combine <- function(ps) {
  ps <- ps[is.finite(ps) & ps > 0 & ps <= 1]
  if (length(ps) < 1) return(NA_real_)
  if (length(ps) == 1) return(ps)
  X <- -2 * sum(log(ps))
  1 - pchisq(X, df = 2 * length(ps))
}

pooled <- studies %>%
  group_by(Group, Risk_Family) %>%
  summarise(
    UKB_p    = first(delong_p[Cohort == "UKB"]),
    AoU_p    = first(delong_p[Cohort == "AoU"]),
    fisher_p = fisher_combine(c(first(delong_p[Cohort == "UKB"]),
                                 first(delong_p[Cohort == "AoU"]))),
    pe = list(re_meta(AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    AUC_val = sapply(pe, `[`, "est"),
    AUC_lo  = sapply(pe, `[`, "lo"),
    AUC_hi  = sapply(pe, `[`, "hi")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-pe) %>%
  mutate(Cohort = "Pooled")

# --- Build long row_id factor for plotting ---
all_rows <- bind_rows(
  studies %>% dplyr::select(Group, Risk_Family, Cohort, AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi),
  pooled  %>% dplyr::select(Group, Risk_Family, Cohort, AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)
) %>%
  mutate(Cohort = factor(Cohort, levels = c("UKB","AoU","Pooled")))

all_rows$row_id <- factor(
  paste(as.character(all_rows$Group), all_rows$Cohort, sep = " | "),
  levels = unlist(lapply(group_levels, function(g)
    paste(g, c("UKB","AoU","Pooled"), sep = " | ")))
)
all_rows$row_id <- forcats::fct_rev(all_rows$row_id)
all_rows$y_num  <- as.integer(all_rows$row_id)

# Within-family sub-label shown on the y-axis (the family itself sits in the
# strip on the left, so we only need the differentiator within the family).
sub_label <- function(g) dplyr::case_when(
  g == "EAU only"                              ~ "No Short time",
  g == "EAU + Short Time ≤6mo"                 ~ "+ Short Time ≤6mo",
  g == "EAU + Short Time ≤24mo"                ~ "+ Short Time ≤24mo",
  grepl("Short Time ≤6mo$",  g)                ~ "+ Short Time ≤6mo",
  grepl("Short Time ≤24mo$", g)                ~ "+ Short Time ≤24mo",
  grepl("≥[234] events$", g)                   ~ "No Short time",
  TRUE                                          ~ as.character(g)
)
all_rows$sub_label <- sub_label(as.character(all_rows$Group))

studies_plot <- all_rows %>% filter(Cohort != "Pooled")
pooled_plot  <- all_rows %>% filter(Cohort == "Pooled" & is.finite(AUC_val))

diamond_h <- 0.30
diamond_df <- pooled_plot %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  do(data.frame(
    Group       = .$Group,
    Risk_Family = .$Risk_Family,
    Cohort = "Pooled",
    x = c(.$AUC_lo, .$AUC_val, .$AUC_hi, .$AUC_val),
    y = .$y_num + c(0, diamond_h, 0, -diamond_h)
  )) %>%
  ungroup()

# NOTE: per-classifier Fisher-combined DeLong p-values used to be annotated
# on this plot; they have been moved to Supplementary Table 4 to allow ALL
# pairwise comparisons (not just vs EAU only) to be reported.

# Y-axis labels: show the within-family differentiator at every row position.
# Each Group has 3 rows (UKB top, AoU middle, Pooled bottom). Anchoring to
# the AoU row centres the label vertically on the 3-row block.
y_label_lookup <- all_rows %>%
  dplyr::distinct(y_num, sub_label, Cohort) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Cohort == "AoU") %>%   # middle row → label is centred on the block
  dplyr::arrange(y_num)
# Build a label vector indexed by integer y position
y_labels <- rep("", length(levels(all_rows$row_id)))
y_labels[y_label_lookup$y_num] <- y_label_lookup$sub_label

# --- Within-facet group dividers ---
# Each Group occupies 3 consecutive y_num values (UKB / AoU / Pooled). To
# make the boundary between consecutive Groups within a Risk_Family clearer,
# draw a faint horizontal line at y = (top of group below) + 0.5 — i.e. just
# above each non-bottom Group within each facet.
group_dividers <- all_rows %>%
  dplyr::distinct(Risk_Family, Group, y_num) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Risk_Family, Group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(y_max = max(y_num), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Risk_Family) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(y_max), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::slice(-1) %>%        # drop the topmost group in each facet (no divider above it)
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(y_div = y_max + 0.5)

ggplot() +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = 0.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#F4A3A3", alpha = 0.2) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 0.7, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#F6D186", alpha = 0.2) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.7, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#A6D96A", alpha = 0.2) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.7, linetype = "dotdash", colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.7) +

  # Faint horizontal dividers between Groups within each Risk_Family facet.
  geom_hline(data = group_dividers,
             aes(yintercept = y_div),
             colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 1) +

  geom_errorbarh(data = studies_plot,
                 aes(xmin = AUC_lo, xmax = AUC_hi, y = y_num, colour = Cohort),
                 height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(Risk_Family ~ .,
             scales = "free_y", space = "free_y", switch = "y") +
  geom_point(data = studies_plot,
             aes(x = AUC_val, y = y_num, colour = Cohort, shape = Cohort),
             size = 3, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_polygon(data = diamond_df,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = Group, fill = Cohort),
               colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +

  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq_along(y_labels),
                     labels = y_labels,
                     expand = expansion(add = 0.6)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(UKB = "#1b9e77", AoU = "#d95f02"),
                      breaks = c("UKB","AoU"), name = "Cohort") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(UKB = 16, AoU = 17),
                     breaks = c("UKB","AoU"), name = "Cohort") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Pooled = "#7570b3"),
                    breaks = "Pooled", name = NULL,
                    labels = "Pooled (meta-analysis)") +

  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.49, 0.72)) +
  labs(x = "AUC (95% CI)", y = NULL,
     title = "Baseline Risk Stratification vs Baseline ± 'Short Time' ± 'Recurrent' Disease Definitions") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(strip.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
        strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", size = 9),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95"),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        panel.spacing = unit(0.8, "lines"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13))
```

**Figure 2:** Forest plot of area under the receiver-operator-characteristic curve (AUC-ROC) for predictive accuracy of EAU risk stratification for 5-year recurrence.Vertical Dashed line indicates AUC = 0.5 (no difference between high/low risk); vertical dot-dash line indicates AUC = 0.7 (the generally accepted threshold for meaningful clinical difference); horizontal grey lines within each block - indicate a single analysis trio (UKB/AoU/Meta-analysis). Green circle = UK Biobank, Orange triangle = All of Us, Purple diamond = DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis of UKB + AoU. The diamond's horizontal extent indicates the pooled 95% CI. Per-cohort AUCs and 95% CIs come from a patient-level cluster bootstrap (B = 1000 iterations resampling PARTICIPANTS, not transitions) — appropriate because each participant contributes multiple transition-rows in the long-format dataset. Pairwise statistical comparisons across all classifiers (cluster-bootstrap p-values within each cohort, Fisher-combined across cohorts) are reported in **Supplementary Table 4**.

\newpage

## Figure 3

```{r fig3-km-likelihood, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, out.width="100%"}
# --- Load KM-derived 5-year recurrence rates from each cohort ---
ukb_km <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/km_5y_recurrence_by_event_count.csv",
                   check.names = FALSE)
aou_km <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/km_5y_recurrence_by_event_count_aou.csv",
                   check.names = FALSE)

km_df <- rbind(ukb_km, aou_km)

# --- Relabel Group: "Low Risk (1 event)" → "1st Event"; "High Risk (≥2 events)" → "≥2 Events" ---
km_df$risk_group <- dplyr::case_when(
  grepl("1 event",  km_df$Group, ignore.case = TRUE) &
    !grepl("≥2", km_df$Group)                          ~ "1st Event",
  grepl("≥2",  km_df$Group)                            ~ "≥2 Events",
  TRUE                                                       ~ km_df$Group
)

km_df <- km_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Cohort, risk_group,
                pct = rec_5y_pct,
                lo  = rec_5y_lo_pct,
                hi  = rec_5y_hi_pct)

km_df$risk_group <- factor(km_df$risk_group, levels = c("1st Event", "≥2 Events"))
km_df$Biobank    <- factor(km_df$Cohort,     levels = c("UKB", "AoU"))

# --- Annotation text ---
km_df$label_text <- paste0(
  sprintf("%.1f%%", km_df$pct),
  "\n(",
  sprintf("%.1f", km_df$lo), "–",
  sprintf("%.1f", km_df$hi),
  "%)"
)

# --- Relative likelihood within each cohort (rescale 0-100) ---
km_df_scaled <- km_df %>%
  group_by(Biobank) %>%
  mutate(pct_scaled = scales::rescale(pct, to = c(0, 100))) %>%
  ungroup()

# --- DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis on the LOGIT scale ---
re_meta_logit <- function(theta, vi) {
  theta <- unname(theta); vi <- unname(vi)
  k <- length(theta)
  if (k < 1) return(c(theta = NA_real_, se = NA_real_))
  if (k == 1) return(c(theta = theta[1], se = sqrt(vi[1])))
  w_fe <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * theta) / sum(w_fe)
  Q  <- sum(w_fe * (theta - fe_pe)^2)
  C  <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2 <- max(0, (Q - (k - 1)) / C)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * theta) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(theta = pe, se = pse)
}

meta_df <- km_df %>%
  mutate(p    = pct / 100,
         lo_p = lo  / 100,
         hi_p = hi  / 100,
         theta    = log(p    / (1 - p)),
         theta_lo = log(lo_p / (1 - lo_p)),
         theta_hi = log(hi_p / (1 - hi_p)),
         se = (theta_hi - theta_lo) / (2 * 1.96),
         vi = se^2) %>%
  group_by(risk_group) %>%
  summarise(
    pe = list(re_meta_logit(theta, vi)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    theta_meta = sapply(pe, `[`, "theta"),
    se_meta    = sapply(pe, `[`, "se"),
    p_meta     = 1 / (1 + exp(-theta_meta)),
    lo_meta    = 1 / (1 + exp(-(theta_meta - 1.96 * se_meta))),
    hi_meta    = 1 / (1 + exp(-(theta_meta + 1.96 * se_meta))),
    Biobank    = factor("Meta-analysis", levels = c("UKB","AoU","Meta-analysis")),
    pct        = p_meta * 100,
    lo         = lo_meta * 100,
    hi         = hi_meta * 100,
    pct_scaled = scales::rescale(pct, to = c(0, 100)),
    label_text = paste0(
      sprintf("%.1f%%", pct), "\n(",
      sprintf("%.1f", lo), "–", sprintf("%.1f", hi), "%)"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(risk_group, Biobank, pct, lo, hi, pct_scaled, label_text)

df_plot <- bind_rows(
  km_df_scaled %>% dplyr::select(risk_group, Biobank, pct, lo, hi, pct_scaled, label_text),
  meta_df
)
df_plot$Biobank <- factor(df_plot$Biobank, levels = c("UKB", "AoU", "Meta-analysis"))

# --- Plot ---
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = risk_group, y = "5-year recurrence", fill = pct_scaled)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label_text), size = 6, lineheight = 0.85, colour = "black") +
  facet_grid2(Biobank ~ .,
              strip = strip_themed(background_y = elem_list_rect(
                fill = c("#FFFFFF","#FFFFFF","#D6EAF8"),
                colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6))) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#A6D96A", mid = "#FFFFF0", high = "#E34A33",
                       midpoint = 50, limits = c(0, 100),
                       name = "Relative likelihood\nwithin Each Biobank (%)") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = "5-Year Cumulative Recurrence Likelihood") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black", size = 12),
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(colour = "black"),
    axis.line.x  = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.4, "lines"),
    strip.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

**Figure 3:** Heatmap of 5-year cumulative recurrence likelihood derived from Kaplan-Meier estimates, stratified by event-count group. *1st Event* = participants at their first stone event (transition 1→2). *≥2 Events* = participants at any subsequent transition (2→3, 3→4, 4→5) pooled. Cells are coloured by relative likelihood within each cohort (rescaled 0-100). The Meta-analysis row is the DerSimonian-Laird random-effects pool of UKB and AoU on the logit scale.

\newpage

# Supplementary Figures

### Supplementary Figure 1: Consort Flow Diagram

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{Supplementary Figure 1.pdf}
\end{center}

\newpage

## Sensitivity-analysis Forest Plot (Full Grid)

#### Supplementary Figure 2: Sensitivity-analysis forest plot — full grid of cutoffs × recurrence definitions

```{r supfig55-forest, fig.width=11, fig.height=16, fig.align="center", out.width="100%"}
# Sensitivity-analysis forest plot \u2014 same idiom as main-text Figure 1 but
# without the filters that restricted Figure 1 to the \u22651 subsequent-event
# definition and the 6-/24-month cutoffs only. Shows every (event \u00d7 cutoff \u00d7
# recurrence-definition) combination.

# --- Load + combine ---
ukb <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/prognostic_accuracy_grid_search_results.csv")
aou <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/prognostic_accuracy_grid_search_results_aou.csv")
aou <- aou[!duplicated(aou[, c("Starting_Point","Time_Cutoff_Months","Recurrence_Definition")]), ]

event_map <- c("Index Date"   = "First Event",
               "Second Event" = "Second Event",
               "Third Event"  = "Third Event",
               "Fourth Event" = "Fourth Event")

make_label <- function(sp, c) {
  e <- event_map[sp]
  if (is.na(c) | c == "NA") e else paste0(e, " (", c, "mo)")
}

ukb$Biobank <- "UK Biobank"
ukb$label   <- mapply(make_label, ukb$Starting_Point, as.character(ukb$Time_Cutoff_Months))
aou$Biobank <- "All of Us"
aou$label   <- mapply(make_label, aou$Starting_Point, as.character(aou$Time_Cutoff_Months))

cols <- c("label","Starting_Point","Time_Cutoff_Months","Recurrence_Definition",
          "AUC_val","AUC_lo","AUC_hi","Biobank")
df <- rbind(ukb[, cols], aou[, cols])
df$AUC_val <- as.numeric(df$AUC_val); df$AUC_lo <- as.numeric(df$AUC_lo); df$AUC_hi <- as.numeric(df$AUC_hi)

# --- DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis on AUC ---
re_meta_auc <- function(est, lo, hi) {
  se <- (hi - lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  ok <- is.finite(se) & se > 0 & is.finite(est)
  est <- est[ok]; se <- se[ok]; vi <- se^2
  k <- length(est)
  if (k < 1) return(c(est = NA_real_, lo = NA_real_, hi = NA_real_))
  if (k == 1) return(c(est = est, lo = est - 1.96*se, hi = est + 1.96*se))
  w_fe <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * est) / sum(w_fe)
  Q  <- sum(w_fe * (est - fe_pe)^2)
  C  <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2 <- max(0, (Q - (k - 1)) / C)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * est) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(est = pe, lo = pe - 1.96 * pse, hi = pe + 1.96 * pse)
}

meta_auc <- df %>%
  mutate(SE = (AUC_hi - AUC_lo) / (2 * 1.96)) %>%
  filter(is.finite(SE) & SE > 0) %>%
  group_by(label, Starting_Point, Time_Cutoff_Months, Recurrence_Definition) %>%
  summarise(pe = list(re_meta_auc(AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    AUC_val = sapply(pe, `[`, "est"),
    AUC_lo  = sapply(pe, `[`, "lo"),
    AUC_hi  = sapply(pe, `[`, "hi"),
    Biobank = "Meta-analysis"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(cols))
df <- rbind(df, meta_auc)

# --- Order ---
event_order <- c("First Event","Second Event","Third Event","Fourth Event")
df$event_label <- event_map[df$Starting_Point]
df$event_label <- factor(df$event_label, levels = event_order)
df$cutoff_num  <- ifelse(is.na(df$Time_Cutoff_Months), 0, as.numeric(df$Time_Cutoff_Months))
df$cutoff_label <- ifelse(df$cutoff_num == 0, " ",
                          paste0(df$Time_Cutoff_Months, " months"))
# Place 6mo on top \u2192 make it the LAST level. Ordering: " ", 24, 21, 18, 15, 12, 9, 6
cutoff_levels <- c(" ", paste0(c(24, 21, 18, 15, 12, 9, 6), " months"))
df$cutoff_label <- factor(df$cutoff_label, levels = cutoff_levels)

df$Biobank <- factor(df$Biobank, levels = c("UK Biobank","All of Us","Meta-analysis"))

rec_labels <- c("\u22651 subsequent stone events" = "\u2265 1 Subsequent Stone Events",
                "\u22652 subsequent stone events" = "\u2265 2 Subsequent Stone Events",
                "\u22653 subsequent stone events" = "\u2265 3 Subsequent Stone Events")
df$rec_facet <- rec_labels[df$Recurrence_Definition]
df$rec_facet <- factor(df$rec_facet, levels = rec_labels)

# --- Studies + meta-analysis split + manual dodge ---
df_studies <- df %>% dplyr::filter(Biobank != "Meta-analysis")
df_meta    <- df %>% dplyr::filter(Biobank == "Meta-analysis")

dodge_w <- 0.7
df_studies$y_num <- as.numeric(df_studies$cutoff_label) +
  dplyr::case_when(
    df_studies$Biobank == "UK Biobank" ~ +dodge_w / 3,
    df_studies$Biobank == "All of Us"  ~  0
  )
df_meta$y_num <- as.numeric(df_meta$cutoff_label) - (dodge_w / 3)

# --- Diamond polygon vertices for meta-analysis ---
diamond_h <- 0.13
diamond_df <- df_meta %>%
  dplyr::rowwise() %>%
  dplyr::do(data.frame(
    label       = .$label,
    event_label = .$event_label,
    rec_facet   = .$rec_facet,
    Biobank     = "Meta-analysis",
    x = c(.$AUC_lo, .$AUC_val, .$AUC_hi, .$AUC_val),
    y = .$y_num + c(0, diamond_h, 0, -diamond_h)
  )) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

y_breaks <- seq_along(levels(df$cutoff_label))
y_labels <- levels(df$cutoff_label)

ggplot() +

  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = 0.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#F4A3A3", alpha = 0.2) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 0.7, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#F6D186", alpha = 0.2) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.7, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#A6D96A", alpha = 0.2) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",  colour = "grey40",   linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.7, linetype = "dotdash", colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.7) +

  geom_errorbarh(data = df_studies,
                 aes(xmin = AUC_lo, xmax = AUC_hi, y = y_num, colour = Biobank),
                 height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(data = df_studies,
             aes(x = AUC_val, y = y_num, colour = Biobank, shape = Biobank),
             size = 3.5, stroke = 0.6) +
  geom_polygon(data = diamond_df,
               aes(x = x, y = y, group = label, fill = Biobank),
               colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +

  facet_grid(event_label ~ rec_facet,
             scales = "free_y", space = "free_y", switch = "y") +

  scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks, labels = y_labels,
                     expand = expansion(add = 0.6)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("UK Biobank" = "#1b9e77",
                                 "All of Us"  = "#d95f02")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("UK Biobank" = 16,
                                "All of Us"  = 17)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Meta-analysis" = "#7570b3"),
                    labels = "Meta-analysis") +

  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.45, 0.75)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.45, 0.75, 0.05)) +

  labs(x = "Area Under Receiver-Operator-Characteristic Curve (95% CI)",
       y = "Short Time Between Events, months",
       title = "EAU Risk Stratification \u2014 Full Sensitivity Analysis",
       subtitle = "Forest Plot of AUC values across all cutoffs and recurrence-event thresholds.\nSplit by number of events at presentation (rows) \u00d7 recurrence definition (columns)") +

  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    strip.text.x      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", size = 9),
    strip.background  = element_rect(fill = "grey95"),
    strip.placement   = "outside",
    panel.spacing     = unit(0.8, "lines"),
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    axis.text.y       = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle     = element_text(size = 10)
  )
```

**Supplementary Figure 2:** Sensitivity-analysis forest plot of AUC for EAU risk stratification across the full grid of starting-event × inter-event-time cutoff × recurrence-definition combinations (cf. main-text Figure 1, which restricts to ≥1 subsequent event and 6-/24-month cutoffs only). Background bands shade AUC < 0.5 (red), 0.5–0.7 (amber) and ≥ 0.7 (green); dashed line at 0.5 = chance discrimination, dot-dash line at 0.7 = generally accepted clinical-usefulness threshold. Green circle = UK Biobank, orange triangle = All of Us, purple diamond = DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis (the diamond's horizontal extent is the pooled 95% CI).


\newpage

## Index Stone Event to First Recurrence

#### Supplementary Figure 3: Index Stone Event → First Recurrence — Unmatched Cohort

Left column: UK Biobank (KM, ROC, DCA top to bottom). Right column: All of Us (KM, ROC, DCA top to bottom).

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UKB – KM}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/km_plot_unmatched_ukb.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) AoU – KM}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/km_plot_unmatched_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.6em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(C) UKB – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/roc_plot_ukb.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(D) AoU – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/roc_plot_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.6em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(E) UKB – DCA}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/dca_unmatched_ukb.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(F) AoU – DCA}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/dca_unmatched_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

#### Supplementary Figure 4: Index Stone Event → First Recurrence — Matched Cohort

Left column: UK Biobank (KM, ROC, DCA top to bottom). Right column: All of Us (KM, ROC, DCA top to bottom). Cohorts matched by age at first stone, sex, EAU risk status and initial presentation.

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UKB – KM}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/km_plot_matched_ukb.pdf}

\hfill

\centering

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}

\textbf{(C) UKB – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/roc_plot_matched_ukb.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(D) AoU – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/roc_plot_matched_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.6em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(E) UKB – DCA}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/dca_matched_ukb.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(F) AoU – DCA}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/dca_matched_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

#### Supplementary Figure 5: Index Stone Event → First Recurrence — Faceted by Initial Presentation/Procedure

Left column: UK Biobank. Right column: All of Us. Rows: KM, ROC, DCA. KM and ROC are faceted by initial procedure; DCA stratified by initial presentation.

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UKB – KM}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Figures/km_plot_facet_ukb.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) AoU – KM}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/km_plot_facet_aou.pdf}

\vspace{0.6em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(C) UKB – ROC}\\[0.3em]

\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(D) AoU – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/roc_plot_facet_by_any_procedure_aou.pdf}

\vspace{0.6em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering

\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering

\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Figures/dca_initial_presentation_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering

\centering

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(C) UKB – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/grid_search_roc_second_date_cutoff_24.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(D) AoU – ROC}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/grid_search_roc_third_event_cutoff_24_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.6em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(E) UKB – DCA}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/grid_search_dca_second_date_cutoff_24.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(F) AoU – DCA}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/grid_search_dca_third_event_cutoff_24_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage
