\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_heatmap_npv_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

### Number of Prior Events Threshold

#### Supplementary Figure 35: Recurrent Disease — KM by Number of Prior Events

Pooled across all transitions. Top row: ≥2 prior events vs 1 event. Bottom row: ≥3 prior events vs <3 prior events. Within each row, (A) = UK Biobank, (B) = All of Us.

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank --- ≥2 vs 1 event}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_2plus.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us --- ≥2 vs 1 event}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_2plus_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank --- ≥3 vs <3 events}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_3plus.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us --- ≥3 vs <3 events}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_3plus_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\end{center}

\newpage

### Number of Prior Events + Time Gaps - KM

#### Supplementary Figure 36: Three-Group Risk — First Event vs Recurrent (Long Gap) vs Recurrent (Short Gap)

Three-group KMs at each inter-event-gap cutoff (rows = 12 / 18 / 24 month gap definitions). (A) = UK Biobank, (B) = All of Us in each row.

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank — 12-month gap}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_three_group_12mo.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us — 12-month gap}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_three_group_12mo_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank — 18-month gap}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_three_group_18mo.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us — 18-month gap}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_three_group_18mo_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank — 24-month gap}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_three_group_24mo.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us — 24-month gap}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_three_group_24mo_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\end{center}

\newpage

#### Supplementary Figure 37: Pooled Transitions — EAU Risk Only vs Combined EAU + Recurrent Disease

Pooled across all transitions. Top row: EAU risk only. Bottom row: combined classifier (EAU OR ≥2 prior events with ≤12-month inter-event gap). (A) = UK Biobank, (B) = All of Us.

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank — EAU risk only}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_eau_only_pooled.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us — EAU risk only}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_eau_only_pooled_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank — EAU + recurrent disease}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_combined_eau_recurrent.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us — EAU + recurrent disease}\\[0.3em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_combined_eau_recurrent_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}

\vspace{0.5em}

\end{center}

\newpage

#### Supplementary Figure 38: Four-Group Risk Breakdown – EAU × Recurrent Disease (12 Month Cutoff)

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_four_group.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_km_four_group_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

### Attenuation Test: Does the Time Restriction Change Discrimination?

Three classifiers on the pooled sample (one row per participant, latest transition): *Any recurrence* (≥2 events), *Recent recurrence* (≥2 events AND gap ≤ cutoff), *Distant recurrence* (≥2 events AND gap > cutoff). Panel (C) is the DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis of AUC, drawn in the same idiom as main-text Figure 1/2 (purple diamond = pooled estimate; bands shade AUC < 0.5 red, 0.5–0.7 amber, ≥ 0.7 green). The italic *p*-value beside each combined classifier's diamond is Fisher's combined test of the per-cohort DeLong p-values for that classifier vs *Any recurrence* (the "no time restriction" baseline).

```{r build-attenuation-meta-panels, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
ukb_att <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_auc_metrics.csv",
                    check.names = FALSE)
aou_att <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_auc_metrics_aou.csv",
                    check.names = FALSE)
ukb_dl  <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_delong.csv",
                    check.names = FALSE)
aou_dl  <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_delong_aou.csv",
                    check.names = FALSE)

ivm_att <- function(est, lo, hi) {
  # DerSimonian-Laird random-effects pool (collapses to FE when tau^2 = 0).
  se <- (hi - lo) / (2 * 1.96)
  ok <- is.finite(se) & se > 0 & is.finite(est)
  est <- est[ok]; se <- se[ok]; vi <- se^2
  k <- length(est)
  if (k == 0) return(c(est = NA_real_, lo = NA_real_, hi = NA_real_))
  if (k == 1) return(c(est = est, lo = est - 1.96*se, hi = est + 1.96*se))
  w_fe <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * est) / sum(w_fe)
  Q  <- sum(w_fe * (est - fe_pe)^2)
  C  <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2 <- max(0, (Q - (k - 1)) / C)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * est) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(est = pe, lo = pe - 1.96 * pse, hi = pe + 1.96 * pse)
}

fisher_combine <- function(ps) {
  ps <- ps[is.finite(ps) & ps > 0 & ps <= 1]
  if (length(ps) < 1) return(NA_real_)
  if (length(ps) == 1) return(ps)
  X <- -2 * sum(log(ps))
  1 - pchisq(X, df = 2 * length(ps))
}

build_attenuation_panel <- function(cut) {
  ukb_c <- ukb_att %>% dplyr::filter(Gap_Cutoff_Months == cut) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(Group = Classifier, Cohort = "UKB",
                     AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)
  aou_c <- aou_att %>% dplyr::filter(Gap_Cutoff_Months == cut) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(Group = Classifier, Cohort = "AoU",
                     AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)
  studies <- bind_rows(ukb_c, aou_c)

  group_levels <- c(
    "Any recurrence (≥2 events)",
    paste0("Recent recurrence (gap ≤", cut, "mo)"),
    paste0("Distant recurrence (gap >", cut, "mo)")
  )
  studies$Group <- factor(studies$Group, levels = group_levels)

  pooled <- studies %>%
    group_by(Group) %>%
    summarise(pe = list(ivm_att(AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(AUC_val = sapply(pe, `[`, "est"),
           AUC_lo  = sapply(pe, `[`, "lo"),
           AUC_hi  = sapply(pe, `[`, "hi"),
           Cohort  = "Pooled") %>%
    dplyr::select(Group, Cohort, AUC_val, AUC_lo, AUC_hi)

  ukb_dlc <- ukb_dl %>% dplyr::filter(Gap_Cutoff_Months == cut)
  aou_dlc <- aou_dl %>% dplyr::filter(Gap_Cutoff_Months == cut)

  fisher_p_recent <- fisher_combine(c(
    ukb_dlc$delong_p[ukb_dlc$Comparison == "Any vs Recent"],
    aou_dlc$delong_p[aou_dlc$Comparison == "Any vs Recent"]
  ))
  fisher_p_distant <- fisher_combine(c(
    ukb_dlc$delong_p[ukb_dlc$Comparison == "Any vs Distant"],
    aou_dlc$delong_p[aou_dlc$Comparison == "Any vs Distant"]
  ))

  fmt_p <- function(p) {
    if (is.na(p)) return("")
    if (p < 0.001) return("Fisher p<0.001")
    paste0("Fisher p=", sprintf("%.3f", p))
  }

  fisher_anno <- pooled %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      Group, AUC_hi,
      label_txt = dplyr::case_when(
        grepl("^Recent",  as.character(Group)) ~ paste0(fmt_p(fisher_p_recent),  " (vs Any)"),
        grepl("^Distant", as.character(Group)) ~ paste0(fmt_p(fisher_p_distant), " (vs Any)"),
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  all_rows <- bind_rows(studies, pooled) %>%
    mutate(Cohort = factor(Cohort, levels = c("UKB","AoU","Pooled")),
           Group  = factor(Group,  levels = group_levels))

  # Within each Group facet the rows are: UKB (top), AoU (middle), Pooled (bottom)
  all_rows$y_num <- as.integer(factor(all_rows$Cohort,
                                       levels = c("Pooled","AoU","UKB")))

  studies_plot <- all_rows %>% dplyr::filter(Cohort != "Pooled")
  pooled_plot  <- all_rows %>% dplyr::filter(Cohort == "Pooled" & is.finite(AUC_val))

  diamond_h <- 0.18
  diamond_df <- pooled_plot %>%
    dplyr::rowwise() %>%
    dplyr::do(data.frame(
      Group  = .$Group,
      Cohort = "Pooled",
      x = c(.$AUC_lo, .$AUC_val, .$AUC_hi, .$AUC_val),
      y = .$y_num + c(0, diamond_h, 0, -diamond_h)
    )) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    mutate(Group = factor(Group, levels = group_levels))

  fisher_anno$y_num <- 1L   # Pooled row within each facet
  fisher_anno$Group <- factor(fisher_anno$Group, levels = group_levels)

  p <- ggplot() +
    annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = 0.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
             fill = "#F4A3A3", alpha = 0.2) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0.5, xmax = 0.7, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
             fill = "#F6D186", alpha = 0.2) +
    annotate("rect", xmin = 0.7, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
             fill = "#A6D96A", alpha = 0.2) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed",  colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.7, linetype = "dotdash", colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbarh(data = studies_plot,
                   aes(xmin = AUC_lo, xmax = AUC_hi, y = y_num, colour = Cohort),
                   height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_point(data = studies_plot,
               aes(x = AUC_val, y = y_num, colour = Cohort, shape = Cohort),
               size = 3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_polygon(data = diamond_df,
                 aes(x = x, y = y, group = Group, fill = Cohort),
                 colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_text(data = fisher_anno,
              aes(x = pmin(AUC_hi + 0.01, 0.95), y = y_num, label = label_txt),
              size = 2.4, hjust = 0, fontface = "italic", colour = "black") +
    facet_grid(Group ~ ., switch = "y") +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(1, 2, 3),
                       labels = c("Pooled", "AoU", "UKB"),
                       expand = expansion(add = 0.6)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(UKB = "#1b9e77", AoU = "#d95f02"),
                        breaks = c("UKB","AoU"), name = "Cohort") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(UKB = 16, AoU = 17),
                       breaks = c("UKB","AoU"), name = "Cohort") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(Pooled = "#7570b3"),
                      breaks = "Pooled", name = NULL,
                      labels = "Pooled (meta-analysis)") +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.4, 1.05)) +
    labs(x = "AUC (95% CI)", y = NULL,
         title = paste0("Gap cutoff = ", cut, " months")) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
    theme(strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold", size = 8),
          strip.background  = element_rect(fill = "grey95"),
          strip.placement   = "outside",
          panel.spacing.y   = unit(0.6, "lines"),
          legend.position   = "bottom",
          axis.text.y       = element_text(size = 8),
          plot.title        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10))

  ggsave(paste0("attenuation_meta_panel_", cut, ".pdf"), plot = p,
         width = 7, height = 5.5)
}

for (.cut in c(12, 18, 24)) build_attenuation_panel(.cut)
```

#### Supplementary Figure 39: Attenuation Test ROC – 12-Month Gap Cutoff

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_roc_cut_12.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_roc_cut_12_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(C) Meta-analysis}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{attenuation_meta_panel_12.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

#### Supplementary Figure 40: Attenuation Test ROC – 18-Month Gap Cutoff

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_roc_cut_18.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_roc_cut_18_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(C) Meta-analysis}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{attenuation_meta_panel_18.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

#### Supplementary Figure 41: Attenuation Test ROC – 24-Month Gap Cutoff

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_roc_cut_24.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/attenuation_test_roc_cut_24_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.31\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(C) Meta-analysis}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{attenuation_meta_panel_24.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

\newpage

### Whole-Dataset Decision Curve Analysis

#### Supplementary Figure 42: Decision Curve Analysis on the entire transitions_long dataset

\begin{center}
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(A) UK Biobank}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{UKB Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_dca.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\hfill
\begin{minipage}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\textbf{(B) All of Us}\\[0.5em]
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{AoU Recurrence paper results/recurrent_disease_whole_dataset_dca_aou.pdf}
\end{minipage}
\end{center}

**Supplementary Figure 1:** Decision curve analysis (DCA) on the same whole-dataset sample used in main-text Figure 2 — every participant contributes one row per transition they reach (1→2, 2→3, 3→4, 4→5), with EAU risk re-ascertained at each transition's starting event. Each panel overlays four classifiers — EAU only, EAU + ≥2 events, EAU + ≥3 events, EAU + ≥4 events — alongside the standard "Treat all" / "Treat none" reference lines. Net Benefit is plotted against threshold probability. (A) UK Biobank, (B) All of Us. DCA is reported per cohort because Net Benefit depends on cohort-specific outcome prevalence and is not amenable to a simple meta-analytic pool.

\newpage

### Summary Plots
#### Supplementary Figure 43: Sensitivity-analysis 5-year recurrence likelihood heatmap

```{r supfig56-heatmap-likelihood, fig.width=14, fig.height=9, out.width="100%"}
# Sensitivity-analysis heatmap — same visual idiom as main-text Figure 3
# (theme_minimal, traffic-light gradient, Biobank rows including a
# Meta-analysis row, strip_themed) but extended over the full grid of
# starting-event × recurrence-definition × risk-group combinations.

ukb <- read.csv("UKB Recurrence paper results/prognostic_accuracy_grid_search_results.csv")
aou <- read.csv("AoU Recurrence paper results/prognostic_accuracy_grid_search_results_aou.csv")
aou <- aou[!duplicated(aou[, c("Starting_Point","Time_Cutoff_Months","Recurrence_Definition")]), ]

# The CSVs have multiple rows per (Starting_Point, Recurrence_Definition) —
# one row per Time_Cutoff_Months grid point. The heatmap groups by
# event × risk-group × recurrence-def, so without filtering all 7 cutoff
# values pile on top of each other in the same cell. Pick a single
# representative cutoff:
#   • Index Date    → NA (no inter-event cutoff applies)
#   • all others    → 12 months (matches the 12-month cutoff used elsewhere
#                     in the recurrent-disease analyses)
ukb <- ukb[is.na(ukb$Time_Cutoff_Months) | ukb$Time_Cutoff_Months == 12, ]
aou <- aou[is.na(aou$Time_Cutoff_Months) | aou$Time_Cutoff_Months == 12, ]

event_map <- c("Index Date"   = "First Stone Event",
               "Second Event" = "Second Stone Event",
               "Third Event"  = "Third Stone Event",
               "Fourth Event" = "Fourth Stone Event")
event_order <- c("First Stone Event","Second Stone Event","Third Stone Event","Fourth Stone Event")

make_heatmap_df <- function(dat, biobank_name) {
  dat$event_label <- event_map[dat$Starting_Point]
  dat$Biobank <- biobank_name
  high <- data.frame(
    event_label = dat$event_label,
    Recurrence_Definition = dat$Recurrence_Definition,
    Biobank    = dat$Biobank,
    risk_group = "High Risk",
    pct = as.numeric(dat$Likelihood_Recurrence_High_Risk_pct),
    lo  = as.numeric(dat$Likelihood_Recurrence_High_Risk_lo),
    hi  = as.numeric(dat$Likelihood_Recurrence_High_Risk_hi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  low <- data.frame(
    event_label = dat$event_label,
    Recurrence_Definition = dat$Recurrence_Definition,
    Biobank    = dat$Biobank,
    risk_group = "Low Risk",
    pct = as.numeric(dat$Likelihood_Recurrence_Low_Risk_pct),
    lo  = as.numeric(dat$Likelihood_Recurrence_Low_Risk_lo),
    hi  = as.numeric(dat$Likelihood_Recurrence_Low_Risk_hi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  rbind(high, low)
}

df <- rbind(make_heatmap_df(ukb, "UKB"), make_heatmap_df(aou, "AoU"))
df$event_label <- factor(df$event_label, levels = rev(event_order))
df$Biobank    <- factor(df$Biobank, levels = c("UKB","AoU"))
df$risk_group <- factor(df$risk_group, levels = c("Low Risk","High Risk"))

rec_labels <- c("≥1 subsequent stone events" = "≥ 1 Subsequent Stone Events",
                "≥2 subsequent stone events" = "≥ 2 Subsequent Stone Events",
                "≥3 subsequent stone events" = "≥ 3 Subsequent Stone Events")
df$rec_facet <- rec_labels[df$Recurrence_Definition]
df$rec_facet <- factor(df$rec_facet, levels = rec_labels)

df$label_text <- paste0(
  sprintf("%.1f%%", df$pct), "\n(",
  sprintf("%.1f", df$lo), "–", sprintf("%.1f", df$hi), "%)"
)

df_scaled <- df %>%
  group_by(Biobank) %>%
  mutate(pct_scaled = scales::rescale(pct, to = c(0, 100))) %>%
  ungroup()

# DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis on the LOGIT scale (matches Figure 3)
re_meta_logit <- function(theta, vi) {
  theta <- unname(theta); vi <- unname(vi)
  k <- length(theta)
  if (k < 1) return(c(theta = NA_real_, se = NA_real_))
  if (k == 1) return(c(theta = theta[1], se = sqrt(vi[1])))
  w_fe <- 1 / vi
  fe_pe <- sum(w_fe * theta) / sum(w_fe)
  Q  <- sum(w_fe * (theta - fe_pe)^2)
  C  <- sum(w_fe) - sum(w_fe^2) / sum(w_fe)
  tau2 <- max(0, (Q - (k - 1)) / C)
  w_re <- 1 / (vi + tau2)
  pe   <- sum(w_re * theta) / sum(w_re)
  pse  <- sqrt(1 / sum(w_re))
  c(theta = pe, se = pse)
}

meta_df <- df %>%
  mutate(p = pct / 100, lo_p = lo / 100, hi_p = hi / 100,
         theta    = log(p    / (1 - p)),
         theta_lo = log(lo_p / (1 - lo_p)),
         theta_hi = log(hi_p / (1 - hi_p)),
         se = (theta_hi - theta_lo) / (2 * 1.96),
         vi = se^2) %>%
  group_by(event_label, risk_group, rec_facet) %>%
  summarise(pe = list(re_meta_logit(theta, vi)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(theta_meta = sapply(pe, `[`, "theta"),
         se_meta    = sapply(pe, `[`, "se"),
         p_meta     = 1 / (1 + exp(-theta_meta)),
         lo_meta    = 1 / (1 + exp(-(theta_meta - 1.96 * se_meta))),
         hi_meta    = 1 / (1 + exp(-(theta_meta + 1.96 * se_meta)))) %>%
  dplyr::select(-pe) %>%
  mutate(
    Biobank    = factor("Meta-analysis", levels = c("UKB","AoU","Meta-analysis")),
    pct        = p_meta  * 100,
    lo         = lo_meta * 100,
    hi         = hi_meta * 100,
    pct_scaled = scales::rescale(pct, to = c(0, 100)),
    label_text = paste0(
      sprintf("%.1f%%", pct), "\n(",
      sprintf("%.1f", lo), "–", sprintf("%.1f", hi), "%)"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(event_label, risk_group, rec_facet, Biobank,
                pct, lo, hi, pct_scaled, label_text)

df_plot <- bind_rows(
  df_scaled %>% dplyr::select(event_label, risk_group, rec_facet, Biobank,
                               pct, lo, hi, pct_scaled, label_text),
  meta_df
)
df_plot$Biobank <- factor(df_plot$Biobank, levels = c("UKB","AoU","Meta-analysis"))

# --- Plot (same idiom as Figure 3) ---
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = risk_group, y = event_label, fill = pct_scaled)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label_text), size = 3, lineheight = 0.85, colour = "black") +
  facet_grid2(Biobank ~ rec_facet,
              strip = strip_themed(background_y = elem_list_rect(
                fill = c("#FFFFFF","#FFFFFF","#D6EAF8"),
                colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6))) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#A6D96A", mid = "#FFFFF0", high = "#E34A33",
                       midpoint = 50, limits = c(0, 100),
                       name = "Relative likelihood\nwithin Each Biobank (%)") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = "5-Year Cumulative Recurrence Likelihood — Sensitivity Analysis",
       subtitle = "Same idiom as Figure 3, extended over the full grid of recurrence definitions and event-count strata.") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x   = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black", size = 11),
    axis.text.y   = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black", size = 9),
    axis.ticks.x  = element_line(colour = "black"),
    axis.line.x   = element_line(colour = "black"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.4, "lines"),
    strip.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.text.y  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey30")
  )
```

**Supplementary Figure 43:** Sensitivity-analysis heatmap of 5-year cumulative recurrence likelihood, in the same visual idiom as main-text Figure 3. Rows = Biobank (UKB / AoU / DerSimonian-Laird random-effects meta-analysis on the logit scale); columns = recurrence definition (≥1, ≥2, ≥3 subsequent stone events). Within each panel, cells show the proportion of high-risk vs low-risk participants who recurred within 5 years, stratified by their starting stone event (1st / 2nd / 3rd / 4th). For non-index transitions a 12-month inter-event cutoff is used to define the high-risk-by-recent-recurrence component; for the index transition no inter-event cutoff applies. Cells are coloured by likelihood rescaled within each cohort (0–100); cell text shows the absolute percentage and 95% CI.
